spec_match.rationale: The budget simulation is entirely client-side simulated and the live support detection script contains a bug where it assumes support without checking the actual header value (which would be null on a plain fetch anyway).

interactive.rationale: The simulation of bandwidth usage and timeline logging is highly interactive and helpful.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb on the main index page matches the concept's budget monitoring simulation.

edge_cases.rationale: Simulates multiple prefetches leading to bandwidth quota exhaustion, which is a great edge case for prefetch budgeting.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the ChromeStatus link for feature 6247959677108224.

design_system.rationale: Properly styled using the design system variables and is responsive.

openQuestions.title: Fix broken support detection script
openQuestions.detail: The support detection script in prefetch-budget-monitor/index.html performs a plain fetch() to window.location.href and reads the response header 'sec-purpose' (which returns null on a plain fetch anyway) but still marks it as supported since it doesn't throw or check for a null value. The support checking should be corrected or removed.

openQuestions.title: Implement real server route for budget monitoring
openQuestions.detail: Currently, the prefetch budget monitor is fully simulated on the client side. It should ideally hit a real endpoint in server.ts that tracks the mock bandwidth budget and responds with slim/blocked payloads, surfacing the actual network behavior.

summary: A great interactive simulation of prefetch bandwidth budgeting with the mandatory ChromeStatus reference now present. The broken support check and lack of a real server route remain open.
